layman_headline: The market is gripped by fear, but none of these setups are worth chasing right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' territory with a score of 25/100, and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.5%, meaning smaller coins (altcoins) are getting crushed. While some coins like ETH and LDO have bounced recently, the charts still look bearish overall — prices are below their key moving averages. The candidates on the watchlist are either in freefall (BONK, NIGHT), already extended after a big run (LDO up 23% in a week), or lack sufficient data. It's a time for patience, not action.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines and keep your cash ready. Watch Ethereum closely — if it pulls back to around $1,800-$1,820, that could present a much better entry point with a clearer risk/reward setup.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate presents significant headwinds. BONK has a chart bias of -2.0 (strongly bearish) with Three Black Crows — a textbook 'do not catch this knife' setup. NIGHT is down 37.5% in a week with no support levels visible and an -84.5% drawdown from ATH. LDO has the best chart (+0.85 bias, Three White Soldiers) but RSI at 59 and +23% in 7 days means the easy money has been made and the risk/reward at 0.38x is terrible. ETH is the most fundamentally sound option with massive TVL backing and RSI at 39 (approaching oversold, which is our most reliable signal at 80% win rate), but it's below all major MAs with a -0.70 chart bias. The 7% weekly bounce suggests it may need to consolidate or pull back before offering a proper swing entry. BTC dominance at 56.5% further argues against alt exposure right now. The disciplined move is to wait.
risk_factors: Extreme Fear sentiment (25) could deepen into capitulation, dragging all alts lower
BTC dominance at 56.5% and rising means capital is flowing out of alts into Bitcoin
No funding rate data available for any candidate, making leverage positioning opaque
watch_list: ETH price action around $1,800 support — a successful test and bounce would be a strong buy signal
Fear & Greed index movement — a spike below 20 could signal capitulation and a contrarian buying opportunity
BTC behavior near $65K-$67K resistance — a clean breakout could rotate capital back into alts
market_context: The market is in Extreme Fear (25/100) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.5%. BTC itself is showing relative strength (+5.3% weekly) while altcoins broadly struggle, creating a hostile environment for alt swing trades.
context_banner: Extreme Fear dominates the market — discipline demands waiting for cleaner setups rather than catching falling knives.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but the bearish chart structure (-0.70) argues for patience until a clearer reversal forms.
Extreme Fear at 25/100 with a flat trend suggests the market hasn't found a bottom yet; historically contrarian but timing is critical.
Bitcoin eyeing a $70K rally could provide the macro tailwind needed to lift alts, but hasn't materialized yet — a reliable news source (67% win rate) worth monitoring.
ETH derivatives reset signals positioning is being cleaned out, which typically precedes a fresh directional move — bullish if combined with spot demand.
LDO's Three White Soldiers is the only bullish chart pattern among candidates, but 0.38x risk/reward and +23% weekly extension make chasing it reckless.
